location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

secondary_archetype: Growth PM (consumer app, ride request flows, membership/loyalty)

level_signals: Base comp range $148K-$185K in SF, consistent with senior IC at Lyft
Leads core team of engineers, designers, data scientists, and marketers
Expected to drive strategic alignment and present to executive levels

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 4+ years in product management, product design or equivalent consultant experience with significant time spent leading consumer facing products.

Natural leadership and ability to make things happen. You manage project ambiguity, complexity, and interdependencies in an organized and structured way, and you are able to define and analyze metrics that inform the success of your products.
Excellent judgment and thoughtfulness about the dynamics of the product ecosystem.
Strong communication skills and intuition for communication strategy at every level -- within teams, within the company, to users, and other audiences.
Ability to conceptualize, prioritize and lead multiple projects for stakeholders.
Detail oriented with superior organizational skills -- balancing multiple projects, deadlines, and requests should be second nature to you.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in membership, subscription, or loyalty program development in consumer-facing products or services preferred

scope.surface: Lyft consumer app — core ride request flows (Golden Path strategy), spanning request and purchase flows, search, pickups and drop offs, post-match and in-trip experiences
primary_metrics_inferred: Business impact from ride request flow improvements
core_responsibilities: Drive strategic alignment by analyzing Lyft's competitive landscape and market position to define an ambitious product vision
Set team goals and success metrics that directly support Lyft's mission
Lead through uncharted product territory by analyzing fragmented data and emerging patterns to define strategic direction
Build a product roadmap to achieve your goals and lead a core team of engineers, designers, data scientists, and marketers to execute on it
Deeply understand the needs and motivations of your customers
Completely own your team's products from ideation, through development, launch, growth, and maintenance
Clearly communicate roadmaps, priorities, experiments, and decisions across a wide spectrum of audiences from partner teams to executive levels

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($148K-$185K base) plus equity and bonus mentioned
Clear product surface and ownership area (Golden Path, core ride request flows)
18 weeks paid parental leave and strong benefits package
Hybrid with flexibility to work from anywhere up to 4 weeks per year
Well-defined cross-functional team (engineering, design, data science, marketing)
red_flags: Hybrid requires 3 specific days in office (Mon, Wed, Thu) — less flexible than typical hybrid arrangements
No visa sponsorship information provided — unclear for candidates requiring sponsorship
JD references 'equivalent consultant experience' as an alternative to PM experience, which may indicate non-traditional candidates are acceptable but the threshold for 'equivalent' is undefined

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title, comp range, and scope of responsibilities)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not directly listed in JD)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; set to 'unclear')
ambiguities: '4+ years in product management, product design or equivalent consultant experience' — unclear whether 4 years of product design or consulting alone satisfies this requirement or if PM experience is strictly required; treated as hard given the unconditional phrasing
Bonus mentioned as part of total comp ('not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits') but no details provided on bonus structure or target
